NVDA.title: Here's How Much You Would Have Made Owning NVIDIA Stock In The Last 15 Years

SIDU.title: Sidus Space Q1 2026 Earnings Call: Complete Transcript

macro_events.event_summary: Long-end Treasury yields are rising ("bond vigilantes" effectively tightening) as hawkish Kevin Warsh assumes the Fed chairmanship, signaling a higher-for-longer rate regime.
primary_drivers: Higher long-end yields raise discount rates, compressing long-duration equity valuations
Steeper curve / higher rates expand net interest margins for banks and insurers
Hawkish Fed leadership reduces expectations of near-term cuts, pressuring rate-sensitive sectors (housing, REITs, utilities)

beneficiaries.rationale: Charles Schwab's earnings are highly levered to short rates via client cash sweep spreads and reduced cash sorting pressure; higher-for-longer directly boosts NIM.
Largest US bank with significant asset sensitivity; higher long yields and steeper curve expand NIM on loan book repricing.
Life insurer with long-duration liabilities; higher long yields improve reinvestment income and reduce GAAP reserve strain.
P&C insurer float is reinvested at higher yields; investment income line directly benefits from rising rates.

losers.rationale: Largest US homebuilder; mortgage rates track 10Y yield, and higher-for-longer kills affordability and order pace. Warsh seen as hawkish on housing.
Realty Income is a bond-proxy net-lease REIT with high leverage and long lease duration; rising long yields directly compress its valuation multiple and raise refinancing costs.
NextEra is a leveraged utility/renewables developer; higher rates raise project financing costs and compress the bond-proxy premium it has historically carried.
Rocket Companies' mortgage origination volumes collapse as the 10Y/mortgage spread keeps 30Y mortgages near 7%+; direct revenue hit.
Extreme-multiple long-duration growth stock; valuation is acutely sensitive to terminal discount rate moves.

headlines: The bond market is already hiking rates as Kevin Warsh takes over as Fed’s new chair
